body:
MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY ECONOMIC GROWTH AND 
RESILIENCE 
M I N U T E S 
 
Thursday, April 16, 2026, 5:00 pm 
Christine Sinclair Community Centre 
3713 Kensington Avenue 
Burnaby, BC V5B 0A7 
 
PRESENT: 
Mayor Mike Hurley, Chair 
Councillor James Wang, Vice Chair, In the Chair 
Councillor Richard T. Lee, Member 
Councillor Maita Santiago, Member 
Brandon Atherley, Royal Bank of Canada 
David Carley, SustainTech Solutions 
Tom Dielschneider, Svante Technologies Inc. 
Carl Funk, Beedie 
Ron Hyrnuik, Bridge Studios 
Arjun Kadaleevanam, Innovation and Economic Dev. Professiona 
Isabel Kolic, Heights Merchants Association 
Jennifer Scott, Vancouver Whitecaps 
Dr. Vidya Vankayala, Gridmod Innovation Technology Advisory Inc. 
Angie Whitfield, Burnaby Board of Trade 
CONSULTANTS: Amanda Fung, Facilitator 
ABSENT: 
John McCarthy, McCarthy Properties 
STAFF: 
Juli Halliwell, Acting Deputy Chief Administrative Officer 
Bob Klimek, Deputy General Manager Finance 
Lee-Ann Garnett, Deputy General Manager Planning & Development 
Karin Hung, Director Strategic Initiatives 
Rajvir Rao, Director Intergovernmental Relations & Public Affairs 
Sarah Alexander, Director Real Estate and Business Operations 
Richard Rowley, Director Finance Revenue Services 
Dan Layng, Chief Licence Inspector 
Charlene Liew, Planner 3 
Chun Nam Law, Planner 2 
Denise Chak, Administrative Officer 1 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes April 16, 2026 
 
 
2 
1. 
CALL TO ORDER 
Councillor Wang, Vice-Chair, called the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic 
Growth and Resilience (the Task Force) meeting to order at 5:34 pm. 
 
2. 
TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT 
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, respectfully acknowledged that the City of Burnaby is 
located on the unceded territories of the xʷməθkʷəy̓ əm (Musqueam), Sḵwx̱ wú7mesh 
(Squamish), səlilwətaɬ (Tsleil-Waututh), and kʷikʷəƛ̓ əm (Kwikwetlem) Peoples. Each 
Nation has distinct histories and distinct traditional territories that fully or partially 
encompass the city. The City encourages you to learn more about the Host Nations 
whose ancestors have occupied and used these lands, including parts of present-day 
Burnaby, for thousands of years. 
 
3. 
ADOPTION OF AGENDA 
3.1 
Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Agenda 
for April 16, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
THAT the agenda for the Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and 
Resilience meeting of April 16, 2026 be adopted, as presented. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 
 
4. 
ADOPTION OF MINUTES 
4.1 
Minutes of the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience 
Meeting held on March 5, 2026 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
THAT the minutes of the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and 
Resilience meeting held on March 5, 2026, be now adopted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY 
 
5. 
WORKSHOP # 6 - STRATEGY TO ACTION 
5.1 
Chair's Address 
Councillor Wang, Vice Chair, provided the following opening remarks: 
“Good evening everyone, and thank you for being here. 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes April 16, 2026 
 
 
3 
Over the first five workshops, we built a shared understanding of Burnaby’s 
economy—its strengths, the forces shaping it, and the strategic economic 
priorities that we believe will guide its future. Tonight marks an important shift 
from setting direction to taking action. 
The question before us now is not what Burnaby wants to achieve, but how we 
make it happen. Strategy only matters if it leads to real decisions, real programs, 
and real progress. 
This workshop is about moving from priorities to action. Building on the strategic 
priorities identified so far, we’ll be focused on defining the concrete steps the City 
can take—where it can lead, where partnerships matter most, and what can 
realistically move forward in the near to medium term. 
As you take part in the discussion, I encourage a practical, solutions-focused 
mindset. We’ll be asking: Can the City materially move the needle on this? Does 
it align with Burnaby’s values and long-term aspirations? And can progress be 
made within a reasonable timeframe? 
Your input tonight will help shape the action agenda that turns strategy into 
impact. With that, I’ll turn it over to the facilitation team to guide us through the 
session.” 
 
5.2 
Review of Strategic Priorities 
A. Fung, Facilitator, provided a recap of Workshop #5. 
 
5.3 
Summary of Engagement Findings 
A. Fung, Facilitator, reviewed the findings from 12 targeted one-on-one 
interviews and 201 survey responses representing a sampling of Burnaby’s 
business community. The findings indicated broad support for the proposed 
strategic priorities and identified opportunities to strengthen resilience, business 
support, and inclusivity. 
 
5.4 
Icebreaker 
A. Fung, Facilitator, led an icebreaker activity in which Task Force members 
identified key actions for success, the sequencing of critical activities, 
coordination requirements, risks related to misalignment or delays, and early 
indicators of progress. 
*Councillor Santiago and A. Whitfield arrived at 5:43 pm 

Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience Meeting    
Minutes April 16, 2026 
 
 
4 
5.5 
Interactive Discussion and Group Activities 
A. Fung, Facilitator, led a series of interactive discussions focused on Burnaby’s 
approach to technology, innovation, and business supports, including streamlined 
processes and potential pilot initiatives and partnerships. 
 
5.6 
Closing Remarks 
In closing, A. Fung, Facilitator, advised a Miro Board will be sent out to capture 
additional outcomes and action ideas, and reminded members and staff that the 
next workshop will be held on April 30, 2026 at 5:00 pm. 
 
6. 
OTHER BUSINESS 
There was no other business brought before the Task Force at this time. 
 
7. 
ADJOURNMENT 
By unanimous consent and without objection, the Task Force meeting adjourned at 8:07 
pm. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Councillor James Wang 
VICE CHAIR 
 
Denise Chak 
ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moves from Economic Strategy to Action
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its seventh workshop on April 30, 2026, continuing work to turn economic priorities into a concrete action plan for the city. The Task Force — made up of councillors, local business leaders, and city staff — is working to define specific steps Burnaby can take to strengthen its economy, improve business support, and build resilience.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 30 workshop or follow Task Force updates to shape Burnaby's economic action plan.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #7 — Strategy to Action (Part 2)

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force moves from economic strategy to action planning
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to present its minutes from an April 16, 2026 workshop to City Council for review. At that workshop, task force members — including local business reps and city staff — shifted focus from setting economic priorities to identifying concrete steps the City can take to support businesses, technology, and innovation.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the task force's final action plan — it could shape future business support programs and city processes in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth — next workshop

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Moves from Economic Strategy to Action Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience is set to hold its seventh workshop on April 30, 2026, where members will review ideas gathered from the previous session and a shared digital board (called a Miro Board) to help turn economic priorities into concrete action steps. The Task Force — made up of local business leaders, city councillors, and staff — is working to build an action plan aimed at strengthening Burnaby's economy and business community.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 30 workshop or follow Task Force updates to shape Burnaby's economic action plan.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mayor's Task Force Workshop #7 — Strategy to Action (Part 2)
